communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: James W. McCord Jr.
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All records related to James Walter McCord Jr. (died June 15, 2017). Mr. McCord served as an FBI special agent and was one of the Watergate burglars. 

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies at FBI Headquarters. Please also conduct a search for records at the Alexandria Records Center. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously-released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

REQUEST FOR FEE WAIVER

I request that all fees related to the processing of this request be waived. As one of the Watergate burglars, James McCord was a key player in a significant moment in American history. And because Mr. McCord was an FBI employee, the Bureau's records are likely to provide the public with significant insight into that moment. I am not making this request for commercial purposes— I am making it so that responsive records will be easily available to students, historians, journalists, and the general public. All responsive documents will be made available to the general public online for free. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges before fulfilling my request.

I would prefer that this request be filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or by CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Nicholas Marritz

requests are processed in the order that they are received. If you have not
received a FOIPA request number, your request is in the process of being
opened at which time it will be assigned a FOIPA request number and
correspondence will be forthcoming.

you receive a comment that your FOIPA request number was not located in the
database, please check back at a later date.

communications.communication:
Re: FOIPA Request No.: 1437109-000

Dear FBI:

In response to my above-numbered FOIA request for records relating to James Walter McCord, Jr., I received the following response:

"In an effort to provide you with responsive records as expeditiously as possible, we are releasing documents from previous requests regarding your subject. We consider your request fulfilled. Since we relied on previous results, additional records potentially responsive to your subject may exist. If this release of previously processed material does
not satisfy your request, you may request an additional search for records."  

I hereby request an additional search for records. Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records for all relevant names, individuals, agencies and companies at FBI Headquarters. Please also conduct a search for records at the Alexandria Records Center. If any potentially responsive records have been destroyed and/or transferred to NARA, then I request copies of the destruction or transfer slips as well as any other documentation relating to, mentioning or describing said transfer or destruction, to include but not be limited to confirmation that the Bureau has no other copies of said records. If previously-released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

Thank you very much.

Most sincerely,

Nicholas Marritz

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL EMAIL] -  RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1437109-000
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Thank you for contacting foipaquestions@fbi.gov.  Please check the status of your FOIPA Request at http://vault.fbi.gov by clicking on “Check Status of Your FOI/PA Request” on the right side of the page, and follow the instructions below.

Check the Status of Your FOIPA Request
If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-0] please enter [1234567-000] into the system. If your FOIPA Number is [1234567-1] please enter [1234567-001] into the system. If you have any questions about the status of your FOIPA request, please e-mail foipaquestions@fbi.gov.

FIND STATUS OF FOIPA- Request statuses are updated weekly

Please enter the whole FOIPA number-Example: [1234567-000]

FOIPA:
Results will show the Request Number, Case Type and Process Description shown below:
FOIPA:
1234567-000

Case Type:
FOIPA

Process Description (Will display the current progress of the request)
The FBI’s FOIPA Program is searching the FBI’s indices for potentially responsive documents.
You may be contacted via formal letter for all fees and/or negotiation issues that may apply.

NOTE: Recent requests are entered into the FOIPA database in the order that they are received. Before you can check the status, you must have received correspondence assigning a FOIPA request number and the information transferred to the online database. Status information is updated weekly. If a request has been closed within the last six months the online database will display the following: The FOIPA number entered has been closed, and appropriate correspondence has been sent to the address on file.

Estimated Dates of Completion
Requests are processed in the order in which they are received through our multi-track processing system.  Requests are divided into two primary tracks--simple (under 50 pages of potentially responsive documents) and complex (over 50 pages of potentially responsive documents).  Complex requests are further divided into medium, large, and extra-large sub-tracks based upon request size.  Simple track requests typically require the least amount of time to process.  Currently, simple track cases average approximately 152 days from the date of receipt for processing.  Our complex requests in the medium processing track are currently averaging 922 days, large processing track are currently averaging approximately 1,964 days, and extra-large processing track are currently averaging 2,360 days for processing.

Respectfully,

Public Information Officer
FBI - Information Management Division
200 Constitution Drive
Winchester, VA  22602
O: (540) 868-4593
E: foipaquestions@fbi.gov<mailto:foipaquestions@fbi.gov>

Do you have further questions about the FOI/PA process? Visit us at http://www.fbi.gov/foia

Please check the status of your request online at https://vault.fbi.gov/fdps-1/@@search-fdps    Status updates are performed on a weekly basis.

Note:  This is a non-emergency email address.  If this is an emergency, please call 911 directly.  If you need to report a tip for immediate action, please contact FBI Tips at http://tips.fbi.gov/ or reach out to your local field office.

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Flock Safety ALPR Audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing from the period of February 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. 
a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data
b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data
c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information
d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (secretary of state)

communications.subject: New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (secretary of state)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: FW: [External] California Public Records Act Request: Math and Statistics courses syllabi (California State University Fullerton)
communications.communication:
Dear Sidanth Grover:
We received your November 30, 2025 Public Records Act request for the following records:
Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

·       All documents and records pertaining to syllabi for Math courses at the University from the last two (2) years.

·       All documents and records pertaining to syllabi for Statistics courses at the University from the last two (2) years.
CSU has conducted a reasonable search for these records under California Government Code §7922.535 and has determined that your request, either in whole or in part, seeks copies of disclosable public records. CSU will make available to you any that are public. Records subject to the following exemptions will be withheld or redacted as appropriate:

  *   Employee records that include personal information, the release of which would constitute an invasion of personal privacy. Cal. Govt. Code §§7927.700, 7928.300, 7922.000.
  *   General Privacy Rights. Cal. Govt. Code §7927.705; Cal. Const. Art. 1, §1.We will let you know when we have gathered all the records.
It will be necessary to redact from them any personal and otherwise privileged information before they can be made available to you. CSU estimates the records will be available January 29, 2026.
If you wish to inspect the records at Cal State Fullerton, you may contact me to schedule a mutually convenient date and time. If you wish to receive copies instead, CSU may charge the direct cost of duplication (20 cents per page). Once the records are located, we will advise you as to the exact number of pages upon which any charge will be based. We will require full payment before we begin the redaction and duplication process. The decision that certain records are exempt and will not be disclosed was made by the Records Access Officer at Cal State Fullerton, Anne Grogan.
Sincerely,
Anne Grogan, ARM (she/her)

Human Resources and Inclusive Excellence
California State University, Fullerton

communications.subject: RE: [External] California Public Records Act Request: Math and Statistics courses syllabi (California State University Fullerton)
communications.communication:
Dear Sidhanth Grover:
On November 30, 2025, we received a request under the California Public Records Act (“CPRA”) for the following records:

·         All documents and records pertaining to syllabi for Math courses at the University from the last two (2) years.

·         All documents and records pertaining to syllabi for Statistics courses at the University from the last two (2) years.
The request is extremely broad, and CSU would like to assist you in making a focused and effective request that reasonably describes identifiable records as required by Government Code section 7922.600.
The search has resulted in more than 17 MB (1200 files, 397 folders) of data, all of which must be carefully and individually reviewed to determine whether it is subject to disclosure under the CPRA. If exemptions apply, the record must be marked for withholding, disclosure, and/or redaction. A secondary review is conducted before redactions are applied, and the records can then be released. This process is extremely time-consuming, and we estimate it will take at least four months to complete.
Such an exhaustive search is not warranted, particularly when it is difficult to discern the public interest involved in the myriad of records that would be responsive to an overly broad and unfocused request. By contrast, the public interest served by not conducting a time-consuming fishing expedition for potentially responsive public records is readily apparent.
Additionally, many of the records that you seek are exempt from production and would be withheld or subject to heavy redaction.

  *   Employee records that include personal information, the release of which would constitute an invasion of personal privacy. Cal. Govt. Code §§7927.700, 7928.300, 7922.000.
  *   General Privacy Rights. Cal. Govt. Code §7927.705; Cal. Const. Art. 1, §1.
Because the search, as currently worded, would result in the review of a high volume of records under the CPRA, the public interest served by conducting the search is minimal and strongly outweighed by the public interest served by not conducting the search (protecting student/employee privacy rights, guarding against the waste of state resources). Govt. Code § 7922.000.
To assist you in making a focused and effective request that reasonably describes identifiable records, CSU provides the following information: Many instructors teach the same class multiple times during the semester, where the syllabi are almost identical to one another, with the exception of the course number.
I propose narrowing the request to one syllabus from each instructor for each class taught during the semester. Additionally, I suggest that we narrow the request to syllabi from Fall 2024 to Fall 2025. If these recommendations do not meet your needs, I invite you to engage in a dialogue to better understand the records you are seeking. Please contact me to schedule a date and time for a phone call.
The decision that certain records are exempt and will not be disclosed was made by the Records Access Officer at Cal State Fullerton, Anne Grogan.
Sincerely,

Anne Grogan, ARM (she/her)
Compliance Manager
Risk Management & Compliance
Human Resources and Inclusive Excellence
California State University, Fullerton
P: 657-278-5465

[cid:image002.png@01DC70DD.11220DC0]

Data on the employers in the state of Minnesota with more than 100 workers receiving benefits from the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), commonly referred to as food assistance.

Specifically, I request the following information, if available:
- The name of the employer
- The total number of the employer's workers who are currently receiving SNAP benefits

communications.subject: FW: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Over the past year, FMCSA has improved the sharing of data with the public through the DOT - Public Data Portal<https://data.transportation.gov/> (Datahub).  By selecting "Trucking & Motorcoaches" you can search our many datasets.  You can use the search catalog in the upper right corner to type in key words like "Carrier" to see what kind of data we share with the public.   You are also able to search Company Census File which contains data for active, inactive an pending entities registered with FMCSA.

The FOIA provides that records made available under the FOIA's proactive disclosure provisions of subsection (a)(1) and (a)(2) are not themselves subject to individualized FOIA requests, see 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(3)(A). This information is already made publicly available through the FMCSA Datahub.

After reviewing the data we share with the public, please let us know if there is something you are unable to find.

Thank you
FMCSA FOIA Office

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Thank you for your response. I wish to significantly narrow this request. Data I seek is daily snapshots of Motor Carrier Census file (also known as Company Census or MCS-150 data) for the period December 2023 through January 2026. I seek this data in CSV, pipe-delimited text, or any machine-readable format.

Please note this archived data is NOT published by the FMCSA, DOT or any third party. I have searched on the Public Data Portal and it is not available. This is data which was once published, and taken offline after it was replaced by a more current version.

Thank you and please let me know if you have questions.

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Recruitment and Hiring (Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting records related to the department's efforts to recruit and hire new officers:
Records include digital and physical, released in their native forms (Excel, PDF, etc)

- ad purchases
- contracts with vendors for ad placement, recruitment and hiring 
- market research and data, if collected
- brand and department strategies created by ad agencies or internally
- data related to applications, KPIs of recruitment and hiring campaigns
- PowerPoint or equivalent presentations, including accompanying documents related to recruitment, hiring, and marketing strategies
- Budgets for recruitment and hiring internally, and the total money spent 
- Pay bonuses or financial incentives to retain officers
- Data by year on the number of officers, sworn and non-sworn

Search from 2021 to today's date.
Please provide in rolling production

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Performance Evaluation (Hopkinton School District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the most recent performance evaluation for the current district superintendent at the time of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

Hello,

The Superintendent of the Hopkinton School District started on July 1, 2025 and does not have a performance evaluation on file.   Thank you for your request; HSD considers this RTK closed.

Thank you,
Laura Taylor
Hopkinton School District

title: Epstein WAVES Records from FOIA 22-18632-F and Aug 12 2014 Screening`

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Epstein WAVES Records from FOIA 22-18632-F and Aug 12 2014 Screening`
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The four (4) WAVES (Worker and Visitor Entry System) records identified as responsive in FOIA 22-18632-F ("Records related to Jeffrey Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell"), which were withheld in part. I request release of the withheld portions, including the specific dates, visitor names, hosts, and visit purposes contained in these records.
2. All visitor clearance requests or Secret Service screening submissions for Jeffrey Epstein, Ghislaine Maxwell, or any known alias, submitted by any White House office during the period January 20, 2009 through January 20, 2017.
3. All visitor clearance requests submitted by Kathryn Ruemmler (White House Counsel, June 2011 - May 2014) for Jeffrey Epstein, Ghislaine Maxwell, or any individual using a known alias of either person.

Background: FOIA 22-18632-F identified 156 responsive assets, of which 4 WAVES records were withheld in part and 152 assets were withheld in full. A DEA OCDETF Fusion Center memorandum (May 2015) documents that on August 12, 2014, both the US Secret Service Uniform Division and the White House Division queried Jeffrey Epstein's NCIC criminal record on the same day. I am seeking to determine whether this NCIC query was related to a planned or actual White House visit by Epstein.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

pest galaxy

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request: Epstein WAVES Records from FOIA 22-18632-F and Aug 12 2014 Screening`
communications.communication:
Dear requester,

The records you are requesting have already been released and are open to 
the public. You may access the records through the Barack Obama 
Presidential Library's Digital Research Room 
<https://www.obamalibrary.gov/digital-research-room> by searching for FOIA 
case number 22-18632-F. The files containing the records are located under 
the Media section of the finding aid 
<https://www.obamalibrary.gov/digital-research-room/finding-aids/records-related-jeffrey-epstein-and-ghislaine-maxwell-22-18632-f>
.  

This concludes our office’s processing of your request.

Sincerely,

Sarah Cain
Archivist & FOIA Coordinator
Barack Obama Presidential Library

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Epstein WAVES Records from FOIA 22-18632-F and Aug 12 2014 Screening`
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Cain,

Thank you for the prompt response. I have downloaded and reviewed all seven ZIP archives from the 22-18632-F finding aid, including the four partially-released WAVES records and all manifest files. I do not contest the WAVES record redactions.

However, my original request contained three items, and items 2 and 3 were not addressed by the existing release:

Item 2 requested all visitor clearance requests or Secret Service screening submissions for Jeffrey Epstein or Ghislaine Maxwell submitted by any White House office during 2009-2017. The WAVES records in 22-18632-F are completed visit entries. Screening submissions — including requests that were denied, withdrawn, or pending — are generated upstream of the WAVES system and are a distinct record category not covered by the existing release.

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: P067024-022526
communications.communication:
Dear Michael Michael: 
Our office received your open records request pursuant to Missouri's Sunshine Law, Chapter 610, RSMo. Your request has been assigned number P067024-022526. The Patrol is committed to adhering to the Sunshine Law and to ensuring that the public obtains access to open records that are not otherwise closed by law. The Sunshine Law requires each public agency to adhere to certain standards. This letter is designed to ensure that you understand our process for fulfilling your records request. Three Business Day Notice Requirement: 
The requested records are not immediately available for dissemination or inspection. The Patrol is required to notify you that additional research time will be required to fulfill the request. This correspondence satisfies the Sunshine Law's notice requirement within the required three business day period. Section 610.023.3, RSMo. 
 Additional Research Time is Required: 
The Patrol receives thousands of open records requests each month. Our Patrol Records Division personnel are dedicated to meeting the intent of Missouri's Sunshine Law and strive to respond to each request in a timely manner. The Patrol operates in all 114 Missouri counties and the independent city of St. Louis. Accordingly, many records maintained by the agency are not centrally located within, or immediately accessible to, the Patrol's Custodian of Records in Jefferson City. Our staff must research your request and ensure that we have captured all responsive records  ̶  regardless of their location within the agency. The research process is time-consuming, but we will make every effort to expedite your request. Estimated Time for Completion: 
The majority of our requests are fulfilled within thirty business days. Some requests require additional research time due to their scope or complexity. Our team will conduct an initial review of your request. If we estimate that the request will be fulfilled within thirty business days, we will not send an updated time estimate. However, if we estimate that the request will take longer than thirty business days, we will provide an updated time estimate that provides a detailed justification for the additional research time. Billing: 
As it relates to billing, Missouri law allows the Patrol to charge fees for copies and research time. Specifically, "[r]esearch time required for fulfilling records requests may be charged at the actual cost of research time." Section 610.026, RSMo. The Missouri Supreme Court recently determined that "staff time is the time required to provide access to public records maintained on computer facilities, recording tapes or disks, videotapes or films, pictures, maps, slides, graphics, illustrations or similar audio or visual items". Gross v. Parson, 624 S.W.3d 877, 884-89 (Mo. 2021) (internal quotations omitted). 
The Patrol will never bill you for redaction time or any research time conducted by our legal staff. You will only be charged for copies and staff research time as permitted by Missouri law. Please let us know if you have questions related to the billing estimate. 
Our team will forward a bill estimate to your attention once we have calculated the permissible costs and fees. The Patrol will not provide the responsive records until receipt of payment. Section 610.026.2, RSMo. 
We appreciate your patience as we review, research, and fulfill your open records request. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you will receive an email when your request has been completed. If you wish to withdraw or clarify your request, or if you have questions, please call our office at (573) 526-6146, or (573) 526-6167. 
 
Sincerely, 
Gerard G. Callahan 
Custodian of Records 
 
Login Link: https://mshp.govqa.us/WEBAPP/_rs/Login.aspx

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Subject: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked) Students (2015–Present)
Dear OBA Custodian of Records:
Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., MuckRock requests this inspection and copies of the following public records:
1.	All applications, determinations, or denials of bar eligibility under Ga. Bar R. 4(c) for Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked) students, 2015–present, including those impacted by SEVIS terminations or immigration status.
2.	Correspondence, memos, or data between OBA and Emory University (Law School/ISSS), DHS/SEVP, or AG on bar access barriers for international students, including 2025 SEVIS effects.
3.	Aggregated, anonymized statistics on bar applicants from Emory/Georgia law schools denied due to status (undocumented, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked)), 2015–present; outcomes/trends; including disparities data vs. ABA policies (e.g., Res. 108, 2017, on undocumented eligibility), empirical evidence of denials/waivers, and communications with Emory on transcript/funding issues.
4.	All OBA/State Bar/Supreme Court reports, statistics, or empirical evidence (e.g., case summaries, waiver denials) on undocumented/visa law student bar eligibility/fitness 2015–2025, including disparities vs. ABA policies (e.g., Res. 108, 2017; 2022 reforms), COVID waivers (Special Rule 2020-1), and communications with Emory on transcript/funding issues.
This disclosure is in the public interest because abrupt loss of lawful status without due process constitutes a severe liberty deprivation under Zadvydas v. Davis, 533 U.S. 678 (2001), governed by the Mathews v. Eldridge, 424 U.S. 319 (1976) balancing test, and recently recognized in Liu v. Noem (D.N.H. 2025) and Kuck Baxter v. DHS (N.D. Ga. 2025).
A complete fee waiver or reduction under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(g) is requested because this request involves a matter of substantial public concern — the deprivation of due process and effective banishment of nonimmigrant students through no-notice SEVIS terminations (see Zadvydas v. Davis, 533 U.S. 678 (2001); Mathews v. Eldridge, 424 U.S. 319 (1976); Liu v. Noem, D.N.H. 2025; Kuck Baxter v. DHS, N.D. Ga. 2025).
Expedited processing is requested under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(2) due to imminent irreparable harm from loss of lawful status and ongoing litigation (Kuck Baxter v. DHS, N.D. Ga. 2025). 
Provide within 3 business days; electronic PDF format preferred. No search fees (Trammell v. Martin, 261 Ga. 1 (1991)).
If any fees are assessed despite the waiver request, please provide an itemized quote; payment will be made via prepaid gift card or U.S. Postal money order to preserve anonymity.
Thank you.
Sincerely,
MuckRock News on behalf of Anonymous Requester

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

neutralrequest47

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmi
communications.communication:
Dear Rebecca S. Mick,
Thank you for your response dated December 9, 2025.
The position that the Office of Bar Admissions falls outside the general scope of the Georgia Open Records Act (“GORA”), O.C.G.A. §§ 50-18-70(a), 50-14-1(a)(1), as interpreted in Fathers Are Parents Too, Inc. v. Hunstein, 202 Ga. App. 716 (1992), and 1979 Op. Att’y Gen. 79-25 because Office of Bar Admissions operates as an arm of the Supreme Court of Georgia is, respectfully, irrelevant for the purposes of this request.
Respectfully, this request seeks administrative and statistical records, not adjudicative case files, and concerns matters of public administration rather than judicial decision-making.
Specifically, the request seeks:
1.	 Aggregated, anonymized statistical data
2.	Administrative correspondence regarding eligibility standards
3.	Policy guidance concerning immigration-status considerations
4.	Reports or empirical summaries concerning bar eligibility determinations
These categories involve the administrative operation of a professional licensing authority rather than confidential adjudicative deliberations in individual fitness cases.
Even where judicial entities are exempt from GORA as courts, Georgia law recognizes a distinction between adjudicative functions and administrative functions. Records reflecting administrative policy development, statistical reporting, or inter-agency communications are not judicial opinions or case files and therefore do not implicate judicial decisional independence.
Importantly, this is not a request for:
1.	Confidential character-and-fitness investigative files of specific applicants
2.	Deliberative materials of pending matters
3.	Personally identifying information of third parties
Rather, the request is limited to:
1.	Aggregate data
2.	Policy-level communications
3.	Administrative reports
4.	Non-case-specific determinations affecting categories of applicants
If the Office maintains that GORA does not apply in any respect, I respectfully request clarification as to whether:
1.	The Supreme Court of Georgia maintains any public-access mechanism for administrative records of the Office of Bar Admissions;
2.	Aggregated statistical data concerning eligibility determinations exists;
3.	Any portion of the request can be processed voluntarily in the interest of transparency.
If the denial rests on confidentiality under the Rules Governing Admission to the Practice of Law (Part F, Section 4), I respectfully note that confidentiality provisions protect individual applicant records and reputational interests; they do not preclude disclosure of anonymized statistical data or general policy communications.
I appreciate your time and look forward to your clarification regarding the proper procedural mechanism for obtaining administrative records, if not under GORA.
Sincerely,
neutralrequest47

I request individual-level inspection records for all inspections conducted by NYSDOT's Passenger Carrier Safety Bureau and Motor Carrier Compliance Bureau for the past 5 years (2021-2026), including:

Inspection date
Operator / carrier name and Operator ID
Vehicle identification (VIN, plate number, vehicle type)
Inspection location (facility, roadside, or terminal)
Inspection result (pass / fail)
Deficiencies found (if any) with category and description
Out-of-service orders issued (if any)
Inspector ID or facility ID
Re-inspection date and result (if applicable)

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request #R010047-021626
communications.communication:
Thank you for the responsive records received today. The Carrier Certification System data (active, suspended and revoked authority) and the individual inspection records (R010047, 11 files covering January 2021 through February 2026) are received and acknowledged.                                

I have one clarification on the inspection records. The R010047 files contain bus and passenger vehicle inspections conducted at operator facilities. Each record joins to an Operator Number assigned to passenger carriers (charter, common, contract). No equivalent inspection records for truck or freight carriers appear in this delivery. 

Could you confirm whether NYSDOT maintains a similar inspection dataset for property/freight carriers? If so, I request those records under the same FOIL request. If NYSDOT does not conduct facility-level inspections for freight    carriers (as distinct from FMCSA roadside inspections), a brief confirmation of that would close this item.

All other responsive records in this delivery appear complete. Thank you for  the prompt response!

communications.subject: RE: New York Freedom of Information Law Request #R010047-021626
communications.communication:
After inspecting the files, I was hoping you could clarify the data:

2025 inspection data appears truncated. The R010047 files for both halves of 2025 contain exactly 100,000 rows each. The H1 file ends on 24-06-2025 (missing 25-30 June) and the H2 file ends on 22-12-2025 (missing 23-31 December). Prior years range from 79,000 to 90,000 rows per half-year, which suggests a row limit was applied during export. Could you re-export both 2025 files without the 100,000-row cap?

Thank you again for your responsiveness.

"Thank you for the responsive records received today. The Carrier Certification System data (active, suspended and revoked authority) and the individual inspection records (R010047, 11 files covering January 2021 through February 2026) are received and acknowledged. I have one clarification on the inspection records. The R010047 files contain bus and passenger vehicle inspections conducted at operator facilities. Each record joins to an Operator Number assigned to passenger carriers (charter, common, contract). No equivalent inspection records for truck or freight carriers appear in this delivery. Could you confirm whether NYSDOT maintains a similar inspection dataset for property/freight carriers? If so, I request those records under the same FOIL request. If NYSDOT does not conduct facility-level inspections for freight carriers (as distinct from FMCSA roadside inspections), a brief confirmation of that would close this item. All other responsive records in this delivery appear complete. Thank you for the prompt response! After inspecting the files, I was hoping you could clarify the data: 2025 inspection data appears truncated. The R010047 files for both halves of 2025 contain exactly 100, 000 rows each. The H1 file ends on 24-06-2025 (missing 25-30 June) and the H2 file ends on 22-12-2025 (missing 23-31 December). Prior years range from 79, 000 to 90, 000 rows per half-year, which suggests a row limit was applied during export. Could you re-export both 2025 files without the 100, 000-row cap? Thank you again for your responsiveness. Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2244206/" 
With respect to the portion of your inquiry asking about " a similar inspection dataset for property/freight carriers", a diligent search of our files failed to reveal any records that are responsive to your request.  To the extent that you are also seeking a statement of information regarding our agency's functions, FOIL is specific to access of agency records and requests for information, answers to questions, or explanations of records provided are not contemplated by the law.  The Committee on Open Government, the New York State agency which provides advisory opinions on FOIL matters, has stated in opinion FOIL-AO-13208, “I note that the title of the [F]reedom of Information Law may be somewhat misleading, for it is not a vehicle that requires agencies to provide information per se; rather, it requires agencies to disclose records to the extent provided by law.” 
With respect to the portion of your inquiry seeking a " re-export both 2025 files without the 100,000-row cap", thank you for making me aware of this specific issue. I have reached out to the program area to request that they repull the 2025 files with an eye towards assuring that the data is not limited by a row cap.  I will release those records to you once the program area has made them available to me. Your records will be released via this, our Records Access Center -- we will not upload documents to an external website. 
Thank you kindly, 
Jonathan Allen 
Records Access Officer 
The New York State Department of Transportation

title: ALPR San Jose Data (San Jose Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: ALPR San Jose Data (San Jose Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The raw number of ALPR cameras (all ALPR cameras, not just those under the brand name “FLOCK”) active in San Jose, CA as of January 1, 2026.
2. The names of agencies and organizations with which the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department shared ALPR data in 2025;
3. The names of agencies and organizations from which the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department received ALPR data in 2025;
4. The names of agencies and organizations with which the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department shared any “hot list” information in 2025;
5. The names of agencies and organizations from which the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department received any “hot list” information in 2025.
6. A definition of the term “hot list” in this context.
7. The aggregate number of "detections" collected during 2025. If the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. For example, most Flock Safety systems may not have this data beyond 30 days.
8. The aggregate number of “hits” during 2025. If the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. For example, most Flock Safety systems may not have this data beyond 30 days.
9. Average Daily Scans based on the past amount of days in which you retain data.
10. A description of the information technology and physical location in which the records exist.
12. Any information or documentation about the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department’s use or potential use of a product called Flock Nova, which combines information from agencies into one place, such as “Unify CAD, RMS, video, LPR, and public records.” It is mentioned in this article (https://www.404media.co/license-plate-reader-company-flock-is-building-a-massive-people-lookup-tool-leak-shows/).
13. Please send a document of the most up-to-date ALPR policy that the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department uses.
14. The location (address, intersection, coordinates, or nearest landmark) of each ALPR camera operated, maintained, or accessed by the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department as of the date of this request.
15. If the City of San Jose and/or the San Jose Police Department uses mobile ALPR cameras (e.g., on patrol cars), please indicate how many mobile units are currently deployed, and describe any policies that govern how locations of scans from mobile units are logged or stored.
16. If exact addresses or coordinates are withheld, please provide general placement descriptions (e.g., "fixed camera at Main St & 2nd Ave," or "mobile ALPR unit assigned to Northern District patrol vehicle").
17. Any internal memos, emails, reports, or presentations from 2022–2025 discussing where ALPR cameras should be placed or were ultimately placed. This includes communications from officers, command staff, city officials, or vendor representatives regarding:
- Placement location recommendations
- Crime statistics or justifications used to determine placement
- Community concerns or opposition
- Equity or privacy impacts
- “High crime” area designations or related targeting rationale

CPRA requires you to undertake reasonable efforts to locate responsive records and to work in good faith with requesters to respond to their request. See CYAC v. City of National City, 220 Cal.App.4th 1385, 1430 (2013). Thus under the CPRA you are obligated to conduct a reasonable search and cannot deny a request merely because it might generate a large volume of records.
We ask that you please respond to this request within 10 days either by providing all the requested records or by providing a written response setting forth the legal authority on which you rely in withholding or redacting any document, as well as stating when documents will be made available. title: CMAP FOIA Request: South La Grange Road Corridor Plan

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: CMAP FOIA Request: South La Grange Road Corridor Plan
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

This is a request under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (5 ILCS 140) for records related to the South La Grange Road Corridor Plan (Project Manager: Todd Vanadilok/Seth Thomas). I am requesting electronic copies of the following:

The full "South La Grange Road Corridor Existing Conditions Report" (and any technical appendices) finalized or presented circa July 2025.

The "Market Study" or "Economic/Real Estate Trends Analysis" associated with this corridor plan (completed circa late 2025/early 2026).

Any raw traffic count data, pedestrian/bicycle volume data, or safety/crash data collected or analyzed by CMAP or its consultants for La Grange Road between 47th Street and Plainfield Road for this project.

Any correspondence or reports regarding infrastructure capacity (specifically water, sewer, and stormwater) as it relates to potential redevelopment along this corridor.

Request Details:

Format: Please provide these records in electronic format (PDF/Excel) via email.

Commercial Purpose: This request is not for a commercial purpose.

Fee Waiver: This information is being requested for the purpose of community advocacy and public safety oversight. I request a waiver of all fees.

Please respond within five (5) business days. Thank you for your transparency and assistance in this planning process.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Concerned Citizen of La Grange

communications.communication:

Sincerely,

Blanca Vela-Schneider
FOIA Officer

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: CMAP FOIA Request: South La Grange Road Corridor Plan
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

Attached is CMAP’s response to your FOIA request dated and received on Friday, March 13, 2026.

The project website includes the PDF links to the Existing Conditions Report and Market Study: https://engage.cmap.illinois.gov/s-la-grange-corridor-plan

Regarding the crash reports the file labeled crashes_la_grange_countryside.csv contains all the crashes within the municipal boundaries of LaGrange and Countryside. The others have filtered subsets that correspond to the counts on page 49 of the Existing Conditions Report. Our staff thought this metadata guide from the Federal Highway Administration’s (Highway Safety Information System Guidebook for the Illinois Data Files) would be helpful to you.  https://highways.dot.gov/sites/fhwa.dot.gov/files/FHWA-HRT-25-112.pdf

Respectfully,

Blanca Vela-Schneider
FOIA Officer

title: Morgan County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Morgan County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Justin Rosa
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
Mar. 12 2026
Kimberly Johnson-Nickles
77 Fairfax St
Berkeley Springs, WV 25441
To whom ever it may concern, 
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to Morgan County opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today.
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from March 1, 2025 through today. 
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, Justin Rosa
Justinrosabo3@gmail.com

With regard to #1, we do not possess a document with this compiled information. However, we do possess a document that may be meaningful to your request. This document provides particular cases (personal information omitted) prosecuted by crime and requested dates by race and several other factors. We will provide this document today. In regard to other information requested, such as date and time of arrest or name and location of the occurrence, although some of the data exists in individual files, creating a document would require manual review of files. The CPRA applies to existing records and does not require a public agency to create a record that does not exist. Further, this request seeks information that constitutes criminal offender record information. See Penal Code Section 13300-13302. The Napa County Superior Court is the custodian of records for docket numbers and they are the appropriate source of that information. communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: Procedures for Transportation of ICE Detainees

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Procedures for Transportation of ICE Detainees
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Transportation procedures and contracts for the transportation of ICE detainees, including but not limited to: 
Policy for the timeline of informing ICE detainees in advance of transfer/deportation
Policy for personal possessions of ICE detainees during transfer
Security policies including use of restraints
Policies for bathroom facilities and food for ICE detainees during transfer 
Contracts pertaining to transportation of ICE detainees

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophia Wright

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Potable Water at the Wyatt Detention Facility
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- All water quality testing results (including for lead, bacteria, and chemical contaminants). 
- Records of water filter installation. 
- Records of inventory of bottled water available without charge to inmates or sold in commissary, including cost of bottled water. 
Please provide responsive document to ANY of the above requests for records, even if records do not exist responsive to all of the requests.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophia Wright

communications.subject: Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act Request: Procedures Pertaining to Inmate Medications
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Rhode Island Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Documentation of procedures pertaining to inmate medications between 2019 and 2026. Including:
- documentation of procedures pertaining to ICE detainee medications upon transfer to another facility
- inmate means of obtaining medication from family members
- inmate methods of obtaining prescriptions from outside providers (including acceptable methods of communication such as fax, mail or telephone)
- how medication is stored
- how medication is administered
- inmate methods of requesting non-prescription medications

Please provide responsive document to ANY of the above requests for records, even if records do not exist responsive to all of the requests.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophia Wright
